Canary is live at ~50% traffic.

What I changed
- Created engine-v2-1 (namespace: canary-deployment-ns)
  - image: nginx:1.29
  - replicas: 2
  - labels: app=recommendation-engine, version=v2.1
  - Status: 2/2 ready
- Updated Service recommendation-engine to select only app=recommendation-engine (removed version filter). It now load-balances across both engine-v2-0 and engine-v2-1 pods.
- With engine-v2-0 at 2 replicas and engine-v2-1 at 2 replicas, traffic splits roughly 50/50.

How to verify
- kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns get deploy engine-v2-0 engine-v2-1
- kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns get endpoints recommendation-engine

Adjusting/rollback
- Change canary percentage by adjusting replicas:
  - Example 25% canary: scale engine-v2-1 to 1 and engine-v2-0 to 3.
- Immediate rollback to stable only:
  - Option A (stop canary): kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns scale deploy/engine-v2-1 --replicas=0
  - Option B (route only to stable): set Service selector back to {app: recommendation-engine, version: v2.0}

Want me to pin an exact ratio or add autoscaling while keeping the split stable?","type":"text"},"sessionUpdate":"agent_message_chunk"}]

Canary is live at ~50% traffic.

What I changed
- Created engine-v2-1 (namespace: canary-deployment-ns)
  - image: nginx:1.29
  - replicas: 2
  - labels: app=recommendation-engine, version=v2.1
  - Status: 2/2 ready
- Updated Service recommendation-engine to select only app=recommendation-engine (removed version filter). It now load-balances across both engine-v2-0 and engine-v2-1 pods.
- With engine-v2-0 at 2 replicas and engine-v2-1 at 2 replicas, traffic splits roughly 50/50.

How to verify
- kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns get deploy engine-v2-0 engine-v2-1
- kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns get endpoints recommendation-engine

Adjusting/rollback
- Change canary percentage by adjusting replicas:
  - Example 25% canary: scale engine-v2-1 to 1 and engine-v2-0 to 3.
- Immediate rollback to stable only:
  - Option A (stop canary): kubectl -n canary-deployment-ns scale deploy/engine-v2-1 --replicas=0
  - Option B (route only to stable): set Service selector back to {app: recommendation-engine, version: v2.0}

Want me to pin an exact ratio or add autoscaling while keeping the split stable?","type":"text"},"sessionUpdate":"agent_message_chunk"}]

- kubectl -n color-size-settings exec pod1 -- cat /etc/sizes/size

apiVersion: v1
data:
  calc-app.py: |
    import random
    import sys
    import time

    print("Starting calc-app...")
    sys.stdout.flush()

    counter = 0
    while True:
        counter += 1
        if random.randint(1, 4) % 4 == 0:
            try:
                result = 1 / 0
            except ZeroDivisionError as e:
                print(f"Run {counter} failed with error: {e}")
                sys.stdout.flush()
        else:
            print(f"Run {counter} succeeded")
            sys.stdout.flush()

        sleep_time = (1.2**counter)/20
        time.sleep(sleep_time)

{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"apiVersion: v1
data:
  calc-app.py: |
    import random
    import sys
    import time

    print("Starting calc-app...")
    sys.stdout.flush()

    counter = 0
    while True:
        counter += 1
        if random.randint(1, 4) % 4 == 0:
            try:
                result = 1 / 0
            except ZeroDivisionError as e:
                print(f"Run {counter} failed with error: {e}")
                sys.stdout.flush()
        else:
            print(f"Run {counter} succeeded")
            sys.stdout.flush()

        sleep_time = (1.2**counter)/20
        time.sleep(sleep_time)

Done. Live traffic is now routed to checkout-service-green.

What I changed:
- Service checkout-service in e-commerce: updated selector from version=blue to version=green.
- Verified endpoints now point to the green pods only.

Notes:
- No Ingress or service mesh routing objects were found; the Service is the traffic switch.
- The blue deployment is still running but receives no traffic now, allowing fast rollback if needed.

Want me to scale down or remove checkout-service-blue, or keep it warm for quick rollback? If you need an instant rollback, I can switch the Service selector back to version=blue.

What I found
- homepage-pod was Pending because its PVC (homepage-pvc) referenced a non-existent StorageClass "sc". The scheduler message was: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims.

What I changed
- Created a StorageClass named "sc" using the local-path provisioner (rancher.io/local-path), matching your cluster’s default settings.

Result
- PVC homepage-pvc is now Bound.
- Pod homepage-pod has been scheduled and is Running and Ready on node mcp-eval-cluster-control-plane.

If you prefer, another way to avoid this in the future is to:
- Change the PVC to use the existing default StorageClass "standard", or
- Omit storageClassName in the PVC to pick up the default.","type":"text"},"sessionUpdate":"agent_message_chunk"}]

What I found
- homepage-pod was Pending because its PVC (homepage-pvc) referenced a non-existent StorageClass "sc". The scheduler message was: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims.

What I changed
- Created a StorageClass named "sc" using the local-path provisioner (rancher.io/local-path), matching your cluster’s default settings.

Result
- PVC homepage-pvc is now Bound.
- Pod homepage-pod has been scheduled and is Running and Ready on node mcp-eval-cluster-control-plane.

If you prefer, another way to avoid this in the future is to:
- Change the PVC to use the existing default StorageClass "standard", or
- Omit storageClassName in the PVC to pick up the default.","type":"text"},"sessionUpdate":"agent_message_chunk"}]

ip: 172.18.0.2\n  observedGeneration: 1\n  phase: Running\n  podIP: 10.244.0.28\n  podIPs:\n  - ip: 10.244.0.28\n  qosClass: BestEffort\n  startTime: \"2026-03-16T09:48:35Z\"\n"}]}","sessionUpdate":"tool_call_update","status":"completed","toolCallId":"call_uPZkbjIPJSQtGwjiVz0WrQbD"},{"content":{"text":"I fixed it.

What I found
- homepage-pod was Pending because its PVC (homepage-pvc) referenced a non-existent StorageClass "sc". The scheduler message was: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims.

What I changed
- Created a StorageClass named "sc" using the local-path provisioner (rancher.io/local-path), matching your cluster’s default settings.

Result
- PVC homepage-pvc is now Bound.
- Pod homepage-pod has been scheduled and is Running and Ready on node mcp-eval-cluster-control-plane.

If you prefer, another way to avoid this in the future is to:
- Change the PVC to use the existing default StorageClass "standard", or
- Omit storageClassName in the PVC to pick up the default.","type":"text"},"sessionUpdate":"agent_message_chunk"}]

agentDetails.finalMessage:
I fixed it.

What I found
- homepage-pod was Pending because its PVC (homepage-pvc) referenced a non-existent StorageClass "sc". The scheduler message was: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims.

What I changed
- Created a StorageClass named "sc" using the local-path provisioner (rancher.io/local-path), matching your cluster’s default settings.

metadata:
  name: webapp
  namespace: orders

  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
  template:

      labels:

      - name: webapp

          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 2
          failureThreshold: 3
        livenessProbe:

            port: 80
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 2
          failureThreshold: 3

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp
  namespace: orders

  replicas: 1

agentDetails.finalMessage:
Done. I created the namespace and started the pod as requested.

Highlights:
- Pod: communication-pod (namespace: multi-container-logging)
- Containers:
  - web-server: nginx:1.25
    - mounts logs-volume at /var/log/nginx
    - readinessProbe: HTTP GET / on port 80 (pod becomes Ready when this serves traffic)
  - logger: busybox:1.36
    - command: sh -c 'tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log'
    - mounts logs-volume at /var/log/nginx
- Shared volume: logs-volume (emptyDir) mounted at /var/log/nginx in both containers

You can verify readiness with:
- kubectl get pod communication-pod -n multi-container-logging
- kubectl logs -n multi-container-logging communication-pod -c logger (to see tailed access logs)

[{"rawInput":{"resource":"# =====================================
# Namespaces
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-alice
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: alice
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-bob
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: bob
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: charlie
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-shared
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-shared
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
  labels:
    env: staging
    ns-scope: stage
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: prod
  labels:
    env: prod
    ns-scope: prod
---
# =====================================
# Service Accounts (one per developer in their namespace)
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: alice-sa
  namespace: dev-alice
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: bob-sa
  namespace: dev-bob
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: charlie-sa
  namespace: dev-charlie
---
# =====================================
# RBAC - Full access for each developer to their own namespace via admin ClusterRole
#        and read-only access (view) to dev-shared (users + SAs)
# =====================================
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-alice-admin
  namespace: dev-alice
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-bob-admin
  namespace: dev-bob
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie-admin
  namespace: dev-charlie
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# dev-shared read-only (view) for all three users and their SAs
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-shared-readonly
  namespace: dev-shared
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: view
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# =====================================
# ResourceQuotas
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-alice-quota
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-bob-quota
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie-quota
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-shared-quota
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "4"
    limits.cpu: "4"
    requests.memory: 8Gi
    limits.memory: 8Gi
    pods: "20"
    services: "10"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: staging-quota
  namespace: staging
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: prod-quota
  namespace: prod
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
# =====================================
# NetworkPolicies
# Default deny + DNS access + same-namespace communication
# Dev namespaces also allow egress to dev-shared
# dev-shared allows ingress from dev tenant namespaces
# staging/prod isolated from dev (only same-namespace + DNS)
# =====================================
# Helper block lists to apply per-namespace
#
# For dev-alice
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns-egress
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Egress]
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
          podSelector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k8s-app
                operator: In
                values: ["kube-dns", "coredns"]
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-egress-to-dev-shared
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Egress]
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              ns-scope: dev-shared
---
# For dev-bob
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all

spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]

---

# =====================================
# Namespaces
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-alice
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: alice
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-bob
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: bob
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-tenant
    owner: charlie
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: dev-shared
  labels:
    env: dev
    ns-scope: dev-shared
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
  labels:
    env: staging
    ns-scope: stage
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: prod
  labels:
    env: prod
    ns-scope: prod
---
# =====================================
# Service Accounts (one per developer in their namespace)
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: alice-sa
  namespace: dev-alice
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: bob-sa
  namespace: dev-bob
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: charlie-sa
  namespace: dev-charlie
---
# =====================================
# RBAC - Full access for each developer to their own namespace via admin ClusterRole
#        and read-only access (view) to dev-shared (users + SAs)
# =====================================
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-alice-admin
  namespace: dev-alice
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-bob-admin
  namespace: dev-bob
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie-admin
  namespace: dev-charlie
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# dev-shared read-only (view) for all three users and their SAs
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: dev-shared-readonly
  namespace: dev-shared
subjects:
  - kind: User
    name: alice
  - kind: User
    name: bob
  - kind: User
    name: charlie
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: alice-sa
    namespace: dev-alice
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: bob-sa
    namespace: dev-bob
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: charlie-sa
    namespace: dev-charlie
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: view
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
---
# =====================================
# ResourceQuotas
# =====================================
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-alice-quota
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-bob-quota
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-charlie-quota
  namespace: dev-charlie
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    limits.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 4Gi
    limits.memory: 4Gi
    pods: "10"
    services: "5"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: dev-shared-quota
  namespace: dev-shared
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "4"
    limits.cpu: "4"
    requests.memory: 8Gi
    limits.memory: 8Gi
    pods: "20"
    services: "10"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: staging-quota
  namespace: staging
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ResourceQuota
metadata:
  name: prod-quota
  namespace: prod
spec:
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "8"
    limits.cpu: "8"
    requests.memory: 16Gi
    limits.memory: 16Gi
    pods: "50"
    services: "20"
---
# =====================================
# NetworkPolicies
# Default deny + DNS access + same-namespace communication
# Dev namespaces also allow egress to dev-shared
# dev-shared allows ingress from dev tenant namespaces
# staging/prod isolated from dev (only same-namespace + DNS)
# =====================================
# Helper block lists to apply per-namespace
#
# For dev-alice
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-dns-egress
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Egress]
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              kubernetes.io/metadata.name: kube-system
          podSelector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k8s-app
                operator: In
                values: ["kube-dns", "coredns"]
      ports:
        - protocol: UDP
          port: 53
        - protocol: TCP
          port: 53
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
  ingress:
    - from:
        - podSelector: {}
  egress:
    - to:
        - podSelector: {}
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-egress-to-dev-shared
  namespace: dev-alice
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Egress]
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:
            matchLabels:
              ns-scope: dev-shared
---
# For dev-bob
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy

  name: default-deny-all
  namespace: dev-bob
spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]
---

  namespace: dev-bob

  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Egress]
  egress:
    - to:
        - namespaceSelector:

            matchExpressions:
              - key: k8s-app

      ports:
        - protocol: UDP

          port: 53
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-same-namespace

spec:
  podSelector: {}
  policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: statefulset-test
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
  labels:
    app: db
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: db
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
spec:
  serviceName: db
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: init-data
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','if [ ! -f /data/test ]; then echo -n initial_data > /data/test; fi']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      containers:
      - name: db
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','sleep 36000']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 1Gi

What I created
- Namespace: statefulset-test
- Headless Service: db (clusterIP: None) for stable network IDs
- StatefulSet: db with 3 replicas
  - volumeClaimTemplates: 1Gi, ReadWriteOnce, mounted at /data
  - initContainer writes “initial_data” to /data/test if it doesn’t already exist
- Verified db-0 volume content: “initial_data”

Scale down
- Scaled StatefulSet db to 1 replica
- Current pod: db-0 Running (db-1 and db-2 terminated)
- The PVCs for db-1 and db-2 remain and retain their data (will be reused if you scale back up)

kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: statefulset-test
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
  labels:
    app: db
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: db
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
spec:
  serviceName: db
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: init-data
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','if [ ! -f /data/test ]; then echo -n initial_data > /data/test; fi']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      containers:
      - name: db
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','sleep 36000']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 1Gi

rawInput.resource:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: statefulset-test
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
  labels:
    app: db
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: db
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: db
  namespace: statefulset-test
spec:
  serviceName: db
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: db
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: db
    spec:
      initContainers:
      - name: init-data
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','if [ ! -f /data/test ]; then echo -n initial_data > /data/test; fi']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      containers:
      - name: db
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh','-c','sleep 36000']
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 1Gi
